channel.title: Meta AI
channel.description: Posts from the Meta AI blog.

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning
Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
Mapping the World's Forests with Greater Precision: Introducing Canopy Height Maps v2
item.description: In partnership with the World Resources Institute, today we’re announcing Canopy Height Maps v2 (CHMv2), an open source model, along with world-scale maps generated with the model.

item.title: Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
item.description: Meta's DINOv2 model is enhancing reforestation efforts around the world. Learn how the UK government is using DINO to help reduce costs and increase access to greenspaces.

item.title: How DINO and SAM are Helping Modernize Essential Medical Triage Practices
item.description: By leveraging advanced AI models, teams at the University of Pennsylvania are aiming to bring cutting-edge automation to emergency response.

item.title: The Universities Space Research Association Applies Segment Anything Model for Responding to Flood Emergencies
item.description: The Universities Space Research Association and Meta are collaborating to help support water observing systems set up by the U.S. Geological Survey.

item.title: Introducing SAM Audio: The First Unified Multimodal Model for Audio Separation
item.description: SAM Audio transforms audio processing by making it easy to isolate any sound from complex audio mixtures using natural, multimodal prompts — whether through text, visual cues, or marking time segments.

item.title: How Orakl Oncology is using DINOv2 to accelerate cancer treatment discovery
item.description: Orakl Oncology aims to accelerate cancer research and drug development by combining experimental, lab-based insights and machine learning.
